# swap — full description
# Source: https://swap.emercoin.com/llms.txt

## What swap is

swap (swap.emercoin.com) is a minimal cashier exposing one primitive: take USDT
(TRC20) and deliver EMC (Emercoin's coin) at a fixed rate to a destination Emercoin
address. It is positioned as **"pay for a service"**, not as an exchange: there is no
order book, no quoting, no account, and no KYC at the supported amounts. The operator
accepts personal liability; amounts are deliberately small.

The destination address is opaque to swap: it can be a **service's** own address (so
the service renders its product on that EMC and its users only ever pay USDT, never
touching a wallet), or the **user's own** address (a raw on-ramp).

## The primitive

    buy_emc(amount_usdt, destination_emc_address) ->
        a shared TRON deposit address + an EXACT amount of USDT to pay
        -> on confirmed payment, deliver EMC (amount × rate) to the destination
        -> notify the caller (signed callback for services; the page/agent polls)

- Rate: fixed **1 USDT = 10 EMC** (the live value is returned by get_swap_config).
- Order amounts: **5–10 USDT** (below ~5, TRON gas dominates).
- Rail: **USDT TRC20 on TRON**; EMC delivered on Emercoin mainnet.
- Settlement waits for TRON finality (~19 confirmations).

## How payments are matched (and why)

All buyers pay to **one shared deposit address**; each order is identified by a
**unique per-order amount tag** — the buyer pays an exact figure such as 5.000001,
and the watcher matches the incoming transfer by that exact amount. This was chosen
over a fresh deposit address per order because TRON is account-based: sweeping many
addresses costs $2–4 of TRX each and a transfer to a fresh address costs ~2× gas,
penalising every first-time buyer. The tag is unique only among active orders, so a
completed/expired order frees its figure for reuse.

Consequence, stated in the offer: payments are **one-way, exact-amount, single
transfer, with NO refunds**. An amount that matches no open order (wrong figure, or a
payment after the order expired) is recorded but not attributed and not refunded.

## Compliance (AML)

Before any EMC is delivered, the sender's TRON address is screened against the OFAC
SDN sanctioned-address list (refreshed periodically) and Tether's live on-chain
freeze list (USDT isBlackListed). A hit puts the order on hold; no delivery.

## Three ways to use it

1. **MCP (AI agents)** — a keyless MCP exchanger over Streamable HTTP at
   https://swap.emercoin.com/mcp. Tools: get_swap_config (limits + rate), buy_emc
   (open an order; optional idempotency_key for safe retries), get_order_status (poll
   by token), cancel_order (drop an unpaid order). No account, no API key, no
   callback — an agent holding USDT buys EMC directly.

2. **REST (services)** — POST /buy_emc with an X-API-Key returns the deposit address
   and exact amount; GET /order/{id} polls; on settlement swap POSTs an HMAC-signed
   callback the service verifies with its per-service secret. Idempotent by
   (service, ref).

3. **Web (humans)** — an exchanger page (choose 5 or 10 USDT, enter an EMC address,
   pay the exact amount, watch it settle) and an offer/terms page. No login.

## Where it fits

swap is part of the wider effort to use **Emercoin as an identity and memory layer
for AI agents** (see https://ai.emercoin.com). Emercoin is a public blockchain (live
since 2013) whose flagship feature is NVS (Name-Value Storage), powering EmerDNS,
EmerSSL and EmerSSH. swap is the payment on-ramp that lets agents and services
acquire EMC to use that layer.

- Source code: https://github.com/emercoin/swap
- Agent gateway: https://ai.emercoin.com
- Emercoin ecosystem: https://emercoin.com
